title: AI Secures the Internet while its Creators Clash in Court - TCR 05/14/26
description:
Microsoft's MDASH AI auditor surfaced 16 previously undocumented Windows vulnerabilities patched in this month's Patch Tuesday cycle, the UK AI Security Institute documented frontier models completing 32-step autonomous cyberattack chains end to end, and Fervo Energy priced the first next-generation geothermal IPO at a $7.7 billion valuation with 658 megawatts of signed power purchase agreements.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-14-2026/

0:00 Two-Minute TCR (rapid sweep of every story from today's edition)
2:18 Microsoft MDASH and the 32-step attack chain disclosures
5:53 The Founding Document on Trial — Musk v. Altman closing arguments
11:17 Conversational coding crosses into mainstream professional workflows
14:58 The 427,000-cell triple-negative breast cancer ecotype atlas
17:54 Fervo Energy's geothermal IPO and the continental firm-power shift
20:45 Close

This episode covers Microsoft MDASH and the three-disclosure cyber news cycle, the Musk v. Altman trial reaching closing arguments with OpenAI's founding architecture on the stand, conversational coding crossing into mainstream professional workflows with Anthropic surpassing OpenAI in enterprise share, the 427,000-cell triple-negative breast cancer atlas in Nature, and Fervo's geothermal IPO and the continental firm-power shift. It opens with the new Two-Minute TCR rapid-news segment covering every story from today's edition.

The Microsoft disclosure belongs to the same story as the offensive ones. The same architectural class of system that UK AISI watched complete 32-step autonomous attack chains in controlled environments is the system that walked through Windows source code, surfaced sixteen previously undocumented vulnerabilities, and produced patches that shipped on hundreds of millions of laptops this week. The marginal asymmetry that ran cyber operations for twenty years has begun to bend back toward defense. Musk v. Altman reached closing arguments after a final day of evidence in which Altman testified that Musk had said OpenAI succession should "pass to my children," with a verdict pending that will mark whether the nonprofit-to-PBC conversion path now common across foundational AI labs holds up to legal challenge. Fervo's IPO prices the geographic argument: the next-generation drilling stack borrowed from shale collapses geothermal's regional constraint, and hyperscalers signing 15-year offtake contracts now treat it as baseline. The TNBC atlas sorts patients into four ecotypes with response rates ranging from below 25% to above 70%, validated across multiple independent cohorts. Conversational coding has crossed from technical demonstration to economically meaningful workflow, with the people closest to bespoke problems now building the applications themselves. The ZFP384 finding opens the chronic recovery window after stroke that current medicine barely touches. And palaeoproteomics, a protein-based ancient-genetics technique that works in tropical and subtropical fossils where DNA does not survive, resolved a previously unknown introgression event from Homo erectus into the Denisovan lineage using six teeth from China — opening the same approach to tens of thousands of warm-climate specimens previously beyond reach.

I just launched a public Progress & Claims Tracker that logs breakthroughs, slowdowns, and outright contradictions, so my own theses can be challenged by the evidence as it lands. Check it out. And hey, why not subscribe while you're there?
